relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The first covenant had service and earthly holy place.
Lampstand, table, and loaves filled the first tent.
Beyond the second veil stood the Holy of Holies.
Ark, manna, Aaron’s rod, and tablets carried covenant memory.
Cherubim overshadowed the mercy-seat.
Priests entered the first tent continually.
The high priest entered the second once yearly with life-offering.
The Spirit showed that the way inward was not yet fully manifested.
The first tent becomes parable for conscience not yet completed.
Regulations stood until a season of setting-straight.
Messiah comes through the greater and more complete tent.
Through his own life he enters once for all and finds age-long release.
Ancient sprinklings sanctified toward fleshly purity.
Messiah’s self-offering cleanses conscience to serve living Divine.
He mediates new covenant and promised inheritance.
Covenant and testament language turns around death.
Death seals the covenant/testament image.
The first covenant was inaugurated with blood/life.
Moses sprinkled scroll and people.
This is blood of covenant commanded by Divine.
Tent and vessels were also sprinkled.
Release is bound to life poured out.
Copies and heavenly realities are distinguished.
Messiah enters heaven itself before Divine’s face for us.
His offering is not repeated year after year.
Once at the ages’ completion he appears to set aside misalignment.
Human life faces death and judgment.
Messiah appears again for those awaiting healing.

contemplative_note: Hebrews 9 is the inner sanctuary of conscience: tabernacle memory opens into Messiah’s once-for-all life-offering and service of the living Divine.
